vm_733_state_at_validation.SOUL_md_GBRAIN_SOUL_ROUTING_V1: marker count=0 (V2 SOUL — anchors absent — correct skip)

cooper_criteria_for_vm_733.3_stepDeployGbrainSoulRouting_ran: DESIGN-CORRECT SKIP (V2 SOUL has no Memory Persistence section; anchors_missing → warnings push → cv bumps. AGENTS.md is the routing surface for V2 SOUL VMs.)

design_finding.issue: Cohort VMs are on SOUL.md V2 template (3KB trimmed, no Memory Persistence section). Edge VMs are on SOUL.md V1 (35K+).
design_finding.implication: v106 stepDeployGbrainSoulRouting was designed for V1 SOUL. For V2 SOUL VMs it correctly no-ops with a warning push (anchors absent). AGENTS.md gbrain content (stepDeployGbrainSoulProtocol) is the routing surface for V2 SOUL VMs.
design_finding.agent_behavior_for_V2_SOUL_VMs: Agent reads SOUL.md V2 (minimal, no Memory Persistence directive) → falls back to AGENTS.md routing → AGENTS.md has gbrain GBRAIN_MEMORY_PROTOCOL_V1 block → agent uses gbrain correctly. No contradiction in SOUL.md to override.
design_finding.phase_2_3_implication: Same pattern. V2 SOUL VMs only get AGENTS.md routing. Acceptable design.
design_finding.edge_vms: Still on V1 SOUL with SOUL_ROUTING_V1 block deployed yesterday. Unaffected.

expected_drain.tomorrow_morning_state_expected: 17/17 with gbrain.service active, AGENTS.md marker present, cv=108
